role: runtime/bridge.py is the HTTP UI face (C8) that serves the operator console and exposes a broad REST/ndjson API over the shared Suite, cognition engine, voice pipeline, and tool registry.
capabilities: HTTP request dispatch and static asset serving via ThreadingHTTPServer
Operator console HTML serving at /studio
Mockup gallery indexing and per-mockup feedback JSONL read/write
Design-system CSS serving
Broad GET API for graph/corpus/state/capabilities introspection
GET API for cognition runs, roles, models, inputs, neighbours, and decisions
GET API for review/journey/voice/personas/trial sessions and image blob serving
POST API for STT, TTS, voice switching, and voice logging
POST API for node CRUD and graph mutations (run, set, move, connect, delete-node)
POST API for conversation, model load/config, mode, and COA management
POST API for surface output/review, idea capture, defer/revive offer, and intent building
POST API for cognition role/skill/context/rule CRUD and run endpoints
Tool listing and invocation with operator gate and consequential-check
POST API for decision/proposal lifecycle, checkpoint, revert, resolve, pin, react
POST API for review/guide/walkthrough/journey/debrief lifecycle
POST API for mockup generation, scale build, registry proposals/approval
Manual activation tick and loadout repoint endpoints
Claude Code turn, greeting, corpus query, and universal projection endpoints
Trial session turn/feedback/reflection endpoints
Channel/session/timeline/board/cascade/flow/routine query and post endpoints
Brain ask, run-in-channel propose, and decision explain/update/propose endpoints
Streaming chat and voice responses over HTTP (ndjson/SSE-style)
Multi-engine TTS routing with Kokoro default and per-sentence streaming
Brain-TTS overlap via producer-consumer queue for low-latency voice
Model context auto-sizing with GPU budget checks and service restart
Semantic, separator, and nucleation projection utilities for meaning-field clustering
Operator token minting and genuine-operator verification
Tool exposure/overlay/posture/gate helpers
Background vector-cache warming, commit-queue drain, and freshness loops
Activation caller integration (dormant-by-default background thread)
substance_why: The file is a ~3,700-line HTTP handler declaring ~100 routes and implementing streaming chat/voice, TTS routing, tool gating, semantic projections, and background loops.
interactions_present: Open the operator console at /studio
Browse and sort the mockup gallery by platform/group and view mockup pages
Read and write per-mockup feedback annotations
View the generated design-system CSS
Inspect graph, corpus, models, layers, types, capabilities, scope, pages, events, and current state
Read chat history and conversations
Send chat messages and receive streaming replies via /api/chat and /api/chat/stream
Start voice turns and receive streamed TTS audio via /api/voice/stream and /api/voice/turn
Submit audio for STT and receive transcriptions
Switch TTS engine/voice and log voice events
Run cognition roles, skills, items, and reduce operations
Create, edit, delete, and dry-run cognition roles, skills, contexts, and rules
Create, update, connect, and delete nodes in the graph
Load and configure models and switch modes
Manage decisions, proposals, resolve, revert, checkpoint, pin, and react
Start and advance review, guide, walkthrough, journey, and debrief sessions
Run trial sessions, submit turns, feedback, and reflections
List available tools and invoke them through the operator-gated door
Capture ideas, defer/revive offers, build intents, and surface output/review
Annotate, apply changes, and write territory labels
Post to channels and query sessions, timeline, board, cascades, flows, and routines
Ask the brain, propose runs in channels, and explain/update/propose decisions
View image blobs by id
Trigger activation tick, loadout repoint, mockup generation, scale build, corpus query, projection, greeting, and Claude Code turn
interactions_missing: Autonomous always-on activation background loop is declared but OFF by default (only /api/activation/tick is wired)
No graceful shutdown or lifecycle management for the server and background threads
No request body size limits or streaming upload parsing visible
No CORS or CSRF handling visible
No rate limiting, connection limits, or thread-pool bounds
No backpressure or bounded queue in the voice streaming producer-consumer loop
No image/attachment upload endpoint (only serving existing blobs by id)
Operator-token enforcement constants exist but may not uniformly gate every POST route
No pagination or caching for feedback JSONL, corpus index, or large graph lists
performance_risks: ThreadingHTTPServer creates a thread per connection with no connection/thread limits, risking exhaustion under many concurrent long streams
Synchronous, monolithic do_GET/do_POST dispatch with a long linear chain of path comparisons
Feedback JSONL is fully read on every GET and appended on every POST, growing unbounded
Corpus index re-enumerates the mockups directory on every request with no caching
Voice streaming uses an unbounded queue for brain parts and WAV chunks, risking memory growth if TTS lags
_stream_parts polls the queue with a 0.4s timeout and probes the socket per sentence, adding overhead
Semantic projection and nucleation math runs in the request thread and can block for large vector sets
Model config restart runs synchronously in the request thread and can stall callers
Image blobs and static files are read fully into memory in request threads
External TTS and Claude calls block request threads with no visible timeouts
reuse_why: It is the canonical UI face and route registry with working endpoints, streaming, and gating, but it is a monolithic 3,700-line mix of transport, business logic, and math that needs structural decomposition rather than replacement.
decomposition_hints: Extract HTTP transport, server startup, and request dispatch into a dedicated server module
Move static asset serving (studio, mockups, CSS, images) into a separate static handler
Split mockup gallery and feedback JSONL API into its own module
Extract voice/TTS streaming, synthesis, and engine routing into a voice service module
Extract chat streaming and ndjson/SSE plumbing into a streaming module
Move cognition role/skill/context/rule CRUD and run endpoints into a cognition API module
Move tool exposure, overlay, posture, gate, and invoke into a tool-face module
Move semantic/projection/nucleation utilities into a meaning-field math module
Move operator token minting and verification into reusable middleware
Move background loops (cache warming, freshness, activation) into a lifecycle module
Move model context sizing and GPU registry ops into an ops/infra module
Split decision/proposal/review/journey/trial lifecycle endpoints into a workflow module
Split node/graph CRUD and Suite state mutations into a graph API module
